sanskrit:
कर्मण्येवाधिकारस्ते मा फलेषु कदाचन।
मा कर्मफलहेतुर्भूर्मा ते सङ्गोऽस्त्वकर्मणि॥
transliteration:
karmaṇy-evādhikāras te mā phaleṣu kadācana |
mā karma-phala-hetur bhūr mā te saṅgo ’stv akarmaṇi ||
literal_meaning: You have authority only over the action, never over its fruits; do not become the agent for the sake of the result, and do not let your attachment be to inaction.
contextual_meaning: Shift attention from uncontrollable outcomes to the controllable process; reduce anticipatory anxiety and performance pressure by anchoring identity in effort, not results.
psychological_state: Hyper-responsibility paralysis: believing one is personally liable for every consequence, leading to avoidance or burnout.
trigger_scenario: A medical resident terrified that any mistake will kill a patient, oscillating between obsessive over-checking charts and fantasies of quitting medicine.
anchor_text: Focus on the work, not the win.
